zh.lifeSummary: 當Maurice FitzGerald de Berminham 2nd Lord of Offaly於1194年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 奧法利時，他的父親Gerald FitzMaurice 1st Lord of Offaly47歲，母親Eve de Bermingham Valognes Lady of Offaly44歲。他於1214年與Juliana Cogan在英國威爾斯米德 格拉莫爾根阿爾達結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1257年5月20日在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克尤加爾享年63歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克。

nameMeanings.name: FitzGerald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Jarrell
meanings: Irish: altered form of   Fitzgerald  .
English (Kent and Middlesex; of Norman origin): variant of Jarrold, itself a variant of Gerald .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Berminham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Sinead.
Irish (of English origin): variant of   Birmingham  . This form of the surname is found mainly in Ireland.

History: The Bermingham family take their surname from lands held at what is now a great city in the West Midlands of England, but it was brought to Ireland as early as the 12th century. The Norman baron Robert de Bermyngeham was a follower of Strongbow (see Clare ); his name is found on a number of Irish charters between 1175 and 1179. The family was also known as Mac Fheorais (see Corish ) in Ireland, named for Piers (Gaelic Feoras) de Bermyngeham, the father of Robert.

variants.name: Birmingham
meanings: English: habitational name from Birmingham in the West Midlands. In Domesday Book the name is already found as Bermingeham, but it seems likely that it was originally Beornmundingahām ‘homestead (Old English hām) of the people of (-inga-) Beornmund’, a personal name composed of the elements beorn ‘young man, warrior’ + mund ‘protection’. This surname is established in Ireland (see Bermingham ).

variants.name: Corish
meanings: Irish:
shortened form of Mac Fheorais ‘son of Piers’. The name was borne by a Connacht family descended from Piers de Bermingham, second lord of Athenry in Galway (died c. 1309). Compare Bermingham .
shortened form of Mac Muirghis ‘son of Maurice’, a personal name introduced to Ireland by the Normans. Compare Morrissey .

nameMeanings.name: Maurice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Late Latin name Mauricius, a derivative of Maurus (a byname meaning ‘Moor’, i.e. ‘dark, swarthy’), borne by, among others, an early Byzantine emperor ( c.539–602 ). It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was popular in the Middle English period, but was not widely adopted by the nobility and became rare in the 17th century. Between the mid-19th century and the 1940s, it was moderately popular but has since faded again. See also Morris .

en.lifeSummary: When Maurice FitzGerald de Berminham 2nd Lord of Offaly was born in 1194, in County Offaly, Ireland, his father, Gerald FitzMaurice 1st Lord of Offaly, was 47 and his mother, Eve de Bermingham Valognes Lady of Offaly, was 44. He married Juliana Cogan in 1214, in Aberdare, Mid Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died on 20 May 1257, in Youghal, County Cork, Ireland, at the age of 63, and was buried in Cork, County Cork, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maurice FitzGerald de Berminham 2nd Lord of Offaly est né en 1194, à County Offaly, Irlande, son père, Gerald FitzMaurice 1st Lord of Offaly, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Eve de Bermingham Valognes Lady of Offaly, avait 44 ans. Il a épousé Juliana Cogan en 1214, à Aberdare, Mid Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé le 20 mai 1257, à Youghal, County Cork, Irlande, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Cork, County Cork, Irlande.

Er hat 1214 in Aberdare, Mid Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom Juliana Cogan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb am 20. Mai 1257 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Youghal, County Cork, Irland und wurde in Cork, County Cork, Irland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzGerald de Berminham 2nd Lord of Offalyが1194年にアイルランドコウントイ オファリーで生まれた時，父Gerald FitzMaurice 1st Lord of Offalyは47歳，母Eve de Bermingham Valognes Lady of Offalyは44歳であった。1214年にイギリスウェールズミド グラモーガンアバーダアでJuliana Coganと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1257年5月20日にアイルランドコウントイ コルクイオウアルで63歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ コルクコルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maurice FitzGerald de Berminham 2nd Lord of Offaly(이)가 1194년 아일랜드 카운티 파리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerald FitzMaurice 1st Lord of Offaly(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Eve de Bermingham Valognes Lady of Offaly(은)는 44세였다. 그는 1214년 영국 웨일스 미드 글램르건 압르더르에서 Juliana Cogan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1257년 5월 20일 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 요우그할에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 코르크에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maurice FitzGerald de Berminham 2nd Lord of Offaly родился в 1194 г. в местности коунти оффали, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Gerald FitzMaurice 1st Lord of Offaly было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Eve de Bermingham Valognes Lady of Offaly, 44. Он женился на женщине по имени Juliana Cogan в 1214 г. в местности абирдар, мид глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер 20 мая 1257 г. в местности еугэл, коунти корк, Ирландия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности корк, коунти корк, Ирландия.

Se casó con Juliana Cogan en 1214, en Aberdare, Mid Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió el 20 de mayo de 1257, en Youghal, County Cork, Irlanda, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Cork, County Cork, Irlanda.
